{"query":"Pope Leo XIV Peru visit November 2026 Vatican official announcement schedule confirmation","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1250,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.americamagazine.org/news/2026/07/17/peru-uruguay-pope-leo/","title":"Peru and Uruguay prepare for Pope Leo’s upcoming visit - America Magazine","content":"7 hours ago — Pope Leo is likely to visit Uruguay and Argentina in one trip with Peru in late fall 2026. the visit could span eight to 10 days in early ...
“At the end of the meeting, the president-elect expressed her satisfaction with the confirmation of the Holy Father’s visit and stated the future government’s willingness to coordinate with the Church the necessary actions to contribute to the development of this important pastoral event,” the bishops’ conference said.
Balcázar said in a statement published after his June 18 meeting with the pope that the visit could span eight to 10 days in early November, with planned stops in Lima, Chiclayo, Piura, Pucallpa and Cusco.
However, at a working breakfast with journalists hosted by the conference July 15, Cardinal Daniel Sturla of Montevideo said the official schedule of the visit would be confirmed by the Vatican in late July."},{"url":"https://www.romereports.com/en/2026/06/18/confirmed-pope-leo-says-he-will-travel-to-peru-this-coming-november/","title":"Confirmed: Pope Leo says he will travel to Peru this coming November - Rome Reports","content":"The Vatican has not yet issued any official announcement.
According to the Peruvian president, it was the pope who suggested the visit would likely take place in early November.
The trip would likely last up to ten days."},{"url":"https://zenit.org/2026/06/18/perus-president-confirms-the-popes-visit-for-november-2026-heres-everything-we-know/","title":"Peru's President Confirms the Pope's Visit for November 2026: Here's Everything We Know - ZENIT - English","content":"(ZENIT News / Rome, 06.18.2026).- For Pope Leo XIV, the journey planned for November will not simply be another international apostolic visit.
According to Balcázar, the Pope’s route will include Lima, Chiclayo, Piura, Pucallpa in the Amazon region, and Cusco.
Although born in Chicago as Robert Francis Prevost, the future Pope dedicated a substantial part of his ministry to the South American nation.
According to the interim president, Leo XIV addressed the situation during their conversation, expressing hope that the electoral process would proceed peacefully, that conflicts would be avoided, and that democratic outcomes would be respected.
Beyond the papal audience, Peruvian officials met with Cardinal Pietro Parolin and Archbishop Paul Richard Gallagher.
Both sides reportedly expressed satisfaction with the current state of relations between Peru and the Holy See and a desire to deepen cooperation further.
Some observers believe those stages could follow Peru later in November, creating what would become the first major Latin American journey of Leo XIV’s pontificate."},{"url":"https://www.ncronline.org/vatican/pope-leo-xiv-meets-perus-president-discusses-possible-november-visit","title":"Pope Leo XIV meets Peru's president, discusses possible November visit | National Catholic Reporter","content":"Balcázar's visit comes as votes are still being counted in Peru's June 7 runoff presidential election between Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sanchez.
A potential papal trip to South America could likely also include stops in Uruguay and Argentina."},{"url":"https://www.visitapapal.pe/en","title":"Pope Leo XIV in Peru 2026 · Travel Guide | visitapapal.pe","content":"He is expected to return in late 2026 for his first trip as pope, to the country where he served as a bishop.
So far, one thing is confirmed: on 18 June 2026, Pope Leo XIV received Peru's interim president at the Vatican.
After that audience, the president said the visit is expected in the first half of November 2026.
According to Peru's government, five cities are expected: Lima, Chiclayo, Piura, Cusco, and Pucallpa, with possible extra stops.
He's Robert Prevost, who led the diocese of Chiclayo from 2015 to 2023 and holds Peruvian citizenship."}]}

After two decades of service in Peru, Pope Leo XIV plans a return visit this November, including stops in Lima and his former Diocese of Chiclayo. The Pope is expected to make stops in his former diocese of Chiclayo, as well as in Lima (the capital of Peru), Piura, and Pucallpa. Balcázar indicated that the Holy Father would also visit a number of “small towns in the north and also in the jungle.” The visit could last as long as 8 to 10 days, the interim president added. “We spoke for two hours, and he was able to confirm for us that he will be in Peru in the first half of November,” Balcázar told journalists. However, this would not be the first time that the host country has confirmed a visit before an official announcement from the Holy See. Although he clarified that the papal visit is still in the planning stages and has a probability of approximately 80%, he emphasized the pontiff’s strong desire to return to the country.
